feature_name: Hotfix: iOS audio-mode native media controls buried under background art (v1.10.1)
regression_of: v1.10.0 Feature 1 (audio thumbnail-as-background art), commit e8754f8, task T2
description: REGRESSION HOTFIX. v1.10.0 Feature 1 added an #audio-bg-art layer behind a transparent audio <video> in .audio-mode. On iOS the album art shows (good) but the in-page native media controls are gone/untappable (Dean confirmed on-device: audio plays + art shows, no tappable controls). ROOT CAUSE (confirmed in public/css/style.css): classic z-index-without-position bug. .audio-bg-art is position:absolute z-index:0 (POSITIONED). #player-wrapper.audio-mode #media-player is given z-index:1 but is NOT positioned (the base .player-container video rule has no position => position:static), and z-index has NO EFFECT on a static element. So the static video paints in normal flow (before positioned descendants) and the absolutely-positioned art (z-index:0) paints ON TOP, burying the video's native iOS control bar. The existing code comment claiming the art 'sits BELOW #media-player (z-index 0 vs 1)' is WRONG for exactly this reason. FIX is CSS-only + targeted; real verification is Dean's on-device iOS retest (controls tappable AND art still visible behind).
path_scope: CSS-only visual fix in public/css/style.css. Automated gate: build-specialist verify (lint 0, 241-test suite green incl. no-CDN/no-emoji guards). NO two-reviewer/QA gate (per coordinator). Real acceptance = Dean's on-device iOS pass.
npm_env_note: Every node/npm command in this repo needs `export PATH="/home/coder/.local/share/fnm/node-versions/v24.14.0/installation/bin:$PATH"` first (fnm not auto-sourced).

artifacts.exec_plan: docs/exec-plans/completed/2026-07-05-audio-art-and-related.md (v1.10.0 origin of the regression; F1 Design section documents the #audio-bg-art layering)

tasks.title: CSS-only fix: give audio-mode video position:relative so z-index applies; make bg-art non-interactive; correct the stacking comment
tasks.description: Three targeted changes in public/css/style.css only. (1) In `#player-wrapper.audio-mode #media-player` (currently `{ background: transparent; z-index: 1; }`) ADD `position: relative;` so the existing `z-index: 1` actually applies and the video (with its native controls) paints ABOVE the .audio-bg-art layer. KEEP background:transparent and z-index:1. (2) In `.audio-bg-art` ADD `pointer-events: none;` (belt-and-suspenders: the art is decorative and must never intercept taps regardless of stacking). (3) Fix the now-inaccurate block comment above .audio-bg-art: it currently says the art 'Sits BELOW #media-player (z-index 0 vs 1)'. Reword to state that the audio <video> is positioned (position:relative) so its z-index:1 places it ABOVE the art layer, keeping the native controls on top and tappable; the art is pointer-events:none decorative and never intercepts input. DO NOT touch anything else. Verify the overlays still stack above the video: resume/transcode z-index:10, skip z-index:6, speed z-index:20 are separately positioned with higher z-index than the video's z-index:1, so they remain above it (confirm, don't change). DO-NOT-REGRESS: video items never get .audio-mode (video-frame display/object-fit:contain/#000 letterbox untouched); playsinline/webkit-playsinline; v1.2.2 iOS background-audio; lock-screen Media Session — all unchanged. No JS/HTML/test changes needed (this is a pure CSS layering fix); resolveAudioArtUrl + the audio-mode toggle logic are correct as-is.
tasks.done_when: public/css/style.css: `#player-wrapper.audio-mode #media-player` has `position: relative;` added (background:transparent + z-index:1 retained); `.audio-bg-art` has `pointer-events: none;` added; the block comment above .audio-bg-art corrected to say the positioned video (z-index:1) paints ABOVE the pointer-events:none decorative art. Overlays (resume:10, skip:6, speed:20) confirmed still above the video. No other files changed. Full 241-test suite green (incl. no-CDN/no-emoji guards), lint 0 (no new warnings beyond the 9/11 baseline), PATH export before npm. Dean's on-device iOS retest (controls tappable AND art still visible behind) is the real acceptance arbiter.

history.note: Hotfix intake (v1.10.1). Regression in v1.10.0 Feature 1 (audio thumbnail-as-background art, commit e8754f8): on iOS the album art shows but the in-page native media controls are gone/untappable (Dean confirmed on-device). Root cause diagnosed and CONFIRMED by EM in public/css/style.css:639-656 — classic z-index-without-position: .audio-bg-art is position:absolute z-index:0, but #player-wrapper.audio-mode #media-player gets z-index:1 with NO position (base .player-container video is position:static), so z-index:1 is inert and the absolutely-positioned art paints on top of the static video, burying its native iOS control bar. The existing comment ('Sits BELOW #media-player z-index 0 vs 1') is wrong. Consulted docs/RELIABILITY.md (no conflict — CSS-only visual fix, no error-handling/logging/perf invariants touched). Coordinator authorized a LIGHTWEIGHT path: skip a heavy design stage, go straight to a scoped software-developer task with the exact 3 CSS changes, then build-verify; NO two-reviewer/QA gate (build-specialist verify + Dean's on-device pass is the gate).

Advanced bootstrap -> implementation (lightweight hotfix path, coordinator-authorized; no separate PM/PE/tasks stages for a root-caused one-file CSS fix). Recorded T1 (status=in-progress): the 3 targeted style.css changes (audio-mode video position:relative so z-index:1 applies; .audio-bg-art pointer-events:none; correct the inaccurate stacking comment) with the overlay-stacking confirmation and the full DO-NOT-REGRESS list (video-frame display, playsinline, v1.2.2 background-audio, lock-screen Media Session). Wrote software-developer inbox at .state/inbox/software-developer.md for T1 ONLY. Gate: build-specialist verify (lint 0, 241 suite green incl. no-CDN/no-emoji guards); acceptance = Dean's on-device iOS pass. Awaiting T1 completion, then /prep-build-verify.
